mechanism_narrative: Positive short-term momentum in industrial production indicates expanding output and improving operating leverage, which raises expected earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 108.96 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month growth has nonpositive correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns in walk-forward tests, or if triggered positive signals produce positive returns in less than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.0%, the higher discount-rate burden reduces equity valuation multiples faster than earnings expectations adjust, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 4.0 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS crossings above 4.0% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts combined with a rising federal funds rate identifies a rate-sensitive housing peak; tighter financing then weakens future construction demand and bank credit, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST above its 12-month average and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 2014.0 as of two months before the decision date
regime_hint: late-cycle tightening with elevated housing activity
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-high and FEDFUNDS-rising condition does not underperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns by at least 1 percentage point on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion with a falling unemployment rate signals improving household income and lower recession risk, which supports consumption and earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls at least 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 5.0 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if this joint labor-strength condition is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 55% of triggered observations or has negative mean excess return versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI while the federal funds rate is rising signals inflation pressure that forces tighter policy and lowers real equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month growth accelerates and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 197.8 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-acceleration and FEDFUNDS-rising interaction produces nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 forward returns or a Rank IC below 0.00 in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate resilient final demand and near-term revenue momentum for consumer-facing firms, which lifts earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 353866.0 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS monthly growth crossings are followed by negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if hit rate for positive returns is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth paired with a low unemployment rate identifies late-cycle capacity pressure; profit margins face wage and input-cost compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 4-quarter growth above 3% and UNRATE below 5.2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 11206.1 as of five months before the decision date
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 5.0 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-GDP-growth and low-UNRATE state is followed by average 60-day S&P 500 returns greater than the unconditional average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening durable-goods demand and tighter credit transmission through construction and mortgages, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 2014.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST 3-month declines below -10% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations.
